---
title: "HW5"
author: "Minh Nguyen"
date: "2026-06-07"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
---



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(skimr)
library(corrplot)

# a. Load Data
df <- read.csv("sp500_companies.csv")

# drop long text column
df <- df %>% select(-Longbusinesssummary)
dim(df)     
str(df)
head(df)

# b. Exploration

skim(df)

ggplot(df, aes(x = fct_infreq(Sector))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Number of Companies per Sector", x = "Sector", y = "Count")

ggplot(df, aes(x = Exchange)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "darkorange") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Stock Exchanges")



df %>%
  select(Currentprice, Marketcap, Ebitda, Revenuegrowth, Fulltimeemployees, Weight) %>%
  gather(key = "variable", value = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Distributions of Numerical Variables")


df %>%
  mutate(
    log_Marketcap = log1p(Marketcap),
    log_Employees = log1p(Fulltimeemployees),
    log_Price     = log1p(Currentprice)
  ) %>%
  select(log_Marketcap, log_Employees, log_Price, Revenuegrowth, Weight) %>%
  gather(key = "variable", value = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Log Transformed Distributions")

# Relationship

# Marketcap by Sector
ggplot(df, aes(x = fct_reorder(Sector, Marketcap, median), y = log1p(Marketcap), fill = Sector)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Market Cap by Sector", x = "Sector", y = "log(Market Cap)")

# Revenue Growth by Sector
ggplot(df, aes(x = fct_reorder(Sector, Revenuegrowth, median, na.rm = TRUE),
               y = Revenuegrowth, fill = Sector)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Revenue Growth by Sector", x = "Sector", y = "Revenue Growth")

# Correlation matrix 
num_df <- df %>%
  select(Currentprice, Marketcap, Ebitda, Revenuegrowth, Fulltimeemployees, Weight) %>%
  drop_na()

cor_matrix <- cor(num_df)
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
         tl.cex = 0.8, addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.7,
         title = "Correlation Matrix", mar = c(0,0,1,0))

# Part c 
# cleaning
library(tidyverse)
colSums(is.na(df))

df$Revenuegrowth[is.na(df$Revenuegrowth)] <- median(df$Revenuegrowth, na.rm = TRUE)

df$Fulltimeemployees[is.na(df$Fulltimeemployees)] <- median(df$Fulltimeemployees, na.rm = TRUE)

df <- df %>%
  group_by(Sector) %>%
  mutate(Ebitda = ifelse(is.na(Ebitda), median(Ebitda, na.rm = TRUE), Ebitda)) %>%
  ungroup()

df$State[is.na(df$State) | df$State == ""] <- "N/A"

cat("Missing values after imputation:\n")
colSums(is.na(df))


par(mfrow = c(2, 3))
boxplot(df$Currentprice,   main = "Current Price",    col = "steelblue")
boxplot(df$Marketcap,      main = "Market Cap",        col = "steelblue")
boxplot(df$Ebitda,         main = "EBITDA",            col = "steelblue")
boxplot(df$Revenuegrowth,  main = "Revenue Growth",    col = "steelblue")
boxplot(df$Fulltimeemployees, main = "Employees",      col = "steelblue")
boxplot(df$Weight,         main = "Weight",            col = "steelblue")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

winsorize <- function(x, lower = 0.01, upper = 0.99) {
  q <- quantile(x, probs = c(lower, upper), na.rm = TRUE)
  x <- ifelse(x < q[1], q[1], x)
  x <- ifelse(x > q[2], q[2], x)
  return(x)
}

df$Currentprice      <- winsorize(df$Currentprice)
df$Marketcap         <- winsorize(df$Marketcap)
df$Ebitda            <- winsorize(df$Ebitda)
df$Revenuegrowth     <- winsorize(df$Revenuegrowth)
df$Fulltimeemployees <- winsorize(df$Fulltimeemployees)

par(mfrow = c(2, 3))
boxplot(df$Currentprice,      main = "Price (cleaned)",     col = "darkorange")
boxplot(df$Marketcap,         main = "Market Cap (cleaned)", col = "darkorange")
boxplot(df$Ebitda,            main = "EBITDA (cleaned)",     col = "darkorange")
boxplot(df$Revenuegrowth,     main = "Rev Growth (cleaned)", col = "darkorange")
boxplot(df$Fulltimeemployees, main = "Employees (cleaned)",  col = "darkorange")
boxplot(df$Weight,            main = "Weight (cleaned)",     col = "darkorange")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

df$Sector   <- as.factor(df$Sector)
df$Industry <- as.factor(df$Industry)
df$Exchange <- as.factor(df$Exchange)
df$Country  <- as.factor(df$Country)
df$State    <- as.factor(df$State)

cat("\nFinal dataset dimensions:", dim(df), "\n")
skim(df)

#part d

# Data Preprocessing 

library(tidyverse)

df_model <- df %>%
  select(-Symbol, -Shortname, -Longname, -City)

df_model <- df_model %>%
  mutate(
    Exchange_NYQ = as.integer(Exchange == "NYQ"),
    Exchange_NGM = as.integer(Exchange == "NGM"),
    Exchange_PCX = as.integer(Exchange == "PCX"),
    
    Country_NonUS = as.integer(Country != "United States"),
    

        State_grouped = fct_lump_n(State, n = 5)
  ) %>%

    mutate(dummy = 1) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = State_grouped, values_from = dummy,
              names_prefix = "State_", values_fill = 0) %>%
  select(-Exchange, -Country, -State)



normalize <- function(x) {
  return((x - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
}

df_model <- df_model %>%
  mutate(
    Currentprice_norm      = normalize(Currentprice),
    Marketcap_norm         = normalize(Marketcap),
    Ebitda_norm            = normalize(Ebitda),
    Revenuegrowth_norm     = normalize(Revenuegrowth),
    Fulltimeemployees_norm = normalize(Fulltimeemployees),
    Weight_norm            = normalize(Weight)
  ) %>%

    select(-Currentprice, -Marketcap, -Ebitda,
         -Revenuegrowth, -Fulltimeemployees, -Weight)

cat("Preprocessed dataset dimensions:", dim(df_model), "\n")
str(df_model)
head(df_model)


df_cluster <- df_model %>%
  select(-Sector, -Industry) %>%
  select(where(is.numeric))

cat("\nClustering dataset dimensions:", dim(df_cluster), "\n")
str(df_cluster)


# part e: clustering 

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(factoextra)  

# elbow method 
set.seed(42)

wss <- map_dbl(1:10, function(k) {
  kmeans(df_cluster, centers = k, nstart = 25)$tot.withinss
})

elbow_df <- data.frame(k = 1:10, wss = wss)

ggplot(elbow_df, aes(x = k, y = wss)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkorange") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(title = "Elbow Method: Choosing Number of Clusters",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "Total Within Cluster SS") +
  theme_minimal()

# Fit K-Means with chosen k

k <- 8

set.seed(42)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(df_cluster, centers = k, nstart = 25)


df_model$Cluster <- as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)

cat("Cluster sizes:\n")
table(df_model$Cluster)

pca_result <- prcomp(df_cluster, scale. = FALSE)  # already normalized

pca_var <- summary(pca_result)$importance[2, 1:5]
cat("\nVariance explained by first 5 PCs:\n")
print(round(pca_var * 100, 2))

pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1     = pca_result$x[, 1],
  PC2     = pca_result$x[, 2],
  Cluster = df_model$Cluster,
  Sector  = df_model$Sector
)

ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  labs(title = "PCA Projection: Colored by K-Means Cluster",
       x = paste0("PC1 (", round(pca_var[1] * 100, 1), "% variance)"),
       y = paste0("PC2 (", round(pca_var[2] * 100, 1), "% variance)")) +
  theme_minimal()


cluster_sector_table <- table(Cluster = df_model$Cluster,
                              Sector  = df_model$Sector)
print(cluster_sector_table)


cluster_sector_df <- as.data.frame(cluster_sector_table)

ggplot(cluster_sector_df, aes(x = Sector, y = Cluster, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Cluster vs Actual Sector",
       fill = "Count")


ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Sector)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  labs(title = "PCA Projection:  Colored by Actual Sector",
       x = paste0("PC1 (", round(pca_var[1] * 100, 1), "% variance)"),
       y = paste0("PC2 (", round(pca_var[2] * 100, 1), "% variance)")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.text = element_text(size = 7))



# Part f Classification

library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)

#  Prepare data for classification

df_class <- df_model %>%
  select(-Cluster, -Industry) %>%
  mutate(Sector = as.factor(Sector))

table(df_class$Sector)

# Train/Test split (80/20)
set.seed(42)
train_index <- createDataPartition(df_class$Sector, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data  <- df_class[train_index, ]
test_data   <- df_class[-train_index, ]

cat("Training rows:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
cat("Testing rows: ", nrow(test_data),  "\n")

# Model 1: Decision Tree
set.seed(42)

cp_grid <- expand.grid(cp = seq(0.001, 0.05, by = 0.005))

dt_model <- train(
  Sector ~ .,
  data      = train_data,
  method    = "rpart",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5),
  tuneGrid  = cp_grid
)

cat("\nBest Decision Tree cp:", dt_model$bestTune$cp, "\n")
plot(dt_model, main = "Decision Tree: CV Accuracy by cp")

rpart.plot(dt_model$finalModel, type = 4, extra = 101,
           main = "Decision Tree", cex = 0.6)

dt_preds <- predict(dt_model, test_data)
dt_cm    <- confusionMatrix(dt_preds, test_data$Sector)
cat("\nDecision Tree Accuracy:", round(dt_cm$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")




# Model 2: Random Forest 
set.seed(42)

rf_grid <- expand.grid(mtry = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10))

rf_model <- train(
  Sector ~ .,
  data      = train_data,
  method    = "rf",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5),
  tuneGrid  = rf_grid,
  ntree     = 200
)

cat("\nBest Random Forest mtry:", rf_model$bestTune$mtry, "\n")
plot(rf_model, main = "Random Forest: CV Accuracy by mtry")

varImpPlot(rf_model$finalModel, main = "Random Forest: Variable Importance",
           n.var = 10, col = "steelblue")

rf_preds <- predict(rf_model, test_data)
rf_cm    <- confusionMatrix(rf_preds, test_data$Sector)
cat("\nRandom Forest Accuracy:", round(rf_cm$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")

# Compare both models
cat("\n Model Comparison \n")
cat("Decision Tree Accuracy: ", round(dt_cm$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")
cat("Random Forest Accuracy: ", round(rf_cm$overall["Accuracy"], 4), "\n")

resamps <- resamples(list(DecisionTree = dt_model, RandomForest = rf_model))
summary(resamps)
bwplot(resamps, metric = "Accuracy", main = "Model Accuracy Comparison")



# Part G: Evaluation

library(caret)
library(pROC)

# Bin sectors into 2 groups

growth_sectors <- c("Technology", "Consumer Cyclical", "Communication Services",
                    "Financial Services", "Industrials")

df_class2 <- df_class %>%
  mutate(Sector_binary = as.factor(
    ifelse(Sector %in% growth_sectors, "Growth", "Defensive")
  ))

table(df_class2$Sector_binary)

# Train/Test split
set.seed(42)
train_index2 <- createDataPartition(df_class2$Sector_binary, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train2 <- df_class2[train_index2, ] %>% select(-Sector)
test2  <- df_class2[-train_index2, ] %>% select(-Sector)

# Rebuild RF on binary
set.seed(42)
rf_binary <- train(
  Sector_binary ~ .,
  data      = train2,
  method    = "rf",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5,
                           classProbs = TRUE,   
                           summaryFunction = twoClassSummary),
  tuneGrid  = expand.grid(mtry = 4),  
  metric    = "ROC",
  ntree     = 200
)

#Predict on test set
rf_preds2  <- predict(rf_binary, test2)
rf_probs2  <- predict(rf_binary, test2, type = "prob")[, "Growth"]

#  Confusion Matrix 
cm2 <- confusionMatrix(rf_preds2, test2$Sector_binary, positive = "Growth")
print(cm2)

cat("\n Confusion Matrix \n")
print(cm2$table)


TP <- cm2$table["Growth", "Growth"]
FP <- cm2$table["Growth", "Defensive"]
FN <- cm2$table["Defensive", "Growth"]
TN <- cm2$table["Defensive", "Defensive"]

precision <- TP / (TP + FP)
recall    <- TP / (TP + FN)
f1        <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
accuracy  <- (TP + TN) / sum(cm2$table)


cat("TP:", TP, " FP:", FP, " FN:", FN, " TN:", TN, "\n")
cat("Precision:", round(precision, 4), "\n")
cat("Recall:   ", round(recall,    4), "\n")
cat("F1 Score: ", round(f1,        4), "\n")
cat("Accuracy: ", round(accuracy,  4), "\n")

# ROC curve 
roc_obj <- roc(test2$Sector_binary, rf_probs2, levels = c("Defensive", "Growth"))

plot(roc_obj,
     col     = "steelblue",
     lwd     = 2,
     main    = paste0("ROC Curve: Random Forest (AUC = ",
                      round(auc(roc_obj), 3), ")"),
     xlab    = "False Positive Rate (1: Specificity)",
     ylab    = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)")
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "gray")

```
